question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted consensusTimestamp to findById() that resolves to a ContractResult with a manipulated gasLimit field, causing downstream fee calculations to use an attacker-controlled gas ceiling and resulting in undercharging of transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger findById() with a consensusTimestamp that maps to a ContractResult whose gasUsed value is zero or negative, causing the fee refund logic in ContractCallService.restoreGasToBucket() to restore an inflated gas amount to the throttle bucket, effectively bypassing gas throttling?
